title: Creating Board from Template via Automation, How do I set correct dates?
excerpt:
I have a Trips Master board. This Board contains an Item for every Trip with a few key details, including dates and status. The Dates column is a Timeline column.

posts.author: horosam (Samuel Horowitz)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have a Trips Master board. This Board contains an Item for every Trip with a few key details, including dates and status. The Dates column is a Timeline column.

When Status is marked Confirmed, I have an Automation that creates a new Trip Tasks Board based on a Trip Tasks template. There are no dates in the Template board since I haven’t found a way to do any kind of dynamic or relative date process. I am driving myself crazy on how to setup Due Dates for these Trip Tasks in the freshly created board.

Some tasks will be due 60 days out from trip start (or end, doesn’t really matter), some a few days out, some due 14 days before trip end, some due 8 days after trip end (the final two do matter).

Simplified view of Boards/Columns

Trips Master

  • Timeline
  • Status

Trip Tasks

  • Link to Trips Master
  • Mirror Timeline from Master
  • Due Date
  • Assignee
  • Dependencies

Trip Task Template

  • Task 1: Book lodging (due 60 days out; assigned to person A)
  • Task 2: Send metrics to boss (due 5 days out; assigned to person B)
  • Task 3: Post report (7 days after; assigned to person A)

posts.author: horosam (Samuel Horowitz)
posts.cookedHtml: ChatGPT offered a way to do this with a formula, and indeed I can create a formula column that gives me the right dates. But how can I set the due date of each task to the “Calculated Date” formula value? It told me to do so with an automation, but I do not have a “Set column value” or “Change column value” automation action.

posts.author: John (John Refford)
posts.cookedHtml: I have the same need @horosam. Did you have any break throughs on this?

posts.author: amytillou (Amy Tillou)
posts.cookedHtml: @horosam , I am eager to hear responses here as well. We’ve recently met with our Monday rep about this exactly. We have projects where we need to automatically calculate a due date for a task based on a launch date, which is possible. However, if the launch date changes, to recalculate due dates, we have to actually change the launch date in every task. I’ve been told there might be a work around but we’re waiting to hear back.
